action.reasoning: Observation: MLP v3 got test IC=0.013348 (new best). Val IC was 0.0281. The gap between val IC (~0.028) and test IC (~0.013) is still large. Previous models: LightGBM ~0.002-0.006, MLP v1 ~0.011, MLP v3 ~0.013. Analysis: The MLP with MSE loss and date-based IC batching is working best. Key issues: 1) Large val-test gap suggests overfitting or distribution shift, 2) Need to try ensemble of multiple models, 3) Could try training with more regularization or different architecture. Decision: Try an ensemble approach combining MLP v3 + LightGBM v3 + Ridge predictions. Also try a deeper MLP with more regularization trained longer. The ensemble should reduce variance and improve generalization. Let me also try training MLP with a different approach - using the raw Alpha158 features without extra CS normalization (since Alpha158 already normalizes), and try a larger model with stronger regularization. I'll create a new MLP v4 with: 1) No extra CS normalization (use Alpha158's built-in), 2) Larger hidden dims [512, 256, 128, 64], 3) Higher dropout 0.5, 4) MSE loss, 5) More epochs with cosine annealing. Also update predict.py to support ensemble mode.
